text.text:
Browned is one of my favorite bakeries! Their cream puffs come in so many different flavors, and every one I've tried has been delicious. The shells are perfectly crispy, the fillings are flavorful, and they're not overly sweet, which I really appreciate. My personal favorites are the chocolate and matcha puffs.

Their croissants are excellent as well—flaky, buttery, and fresh every time. The coffee is also surprisingly good. My favorite drink is the Lemon Breeze coffee, which is refreshing, unique, and pairs perfectly with their pastries.

originalText.text:
Browned is one of my favorite bakeries! Their cream puffs come in so many different flavors, and every one I've tried has been delicious. The shells are perfectly crispy, the fillings are flavorful, and they're not overly sweet, which I really appreciate. My personal favorites are the chocolate and matcha puffs.

Their croissants are excellent as well—flaky, buttery, and fresh every time. The coffee is also surprisingly good. My favorite drink is the Lemon Breeze coffee, which is refreshing, unique, and pairs perfectly with their pastries.

text.text: I got the matcha latte and really liked the flavor, though my boyfriend thought it tasted like grass. The lady working there was very nice and even explained where the matcha comes from and how they make it! We also tried the kimchi bacon garlic roll, which had a very interesting flavor that we both enjoyed. We got the buttery pressed croissant as well, but neither of us liked it—though to be fair, we aren't huge croissant fans anyway, so who knows! Finally, we picked up a couple of cream puffs, and the Biscoff crunch cream puffs were definitely our favorite.

originalText.text: I got the matcha latte and really liked the flavor, though my boyfriend thought it tasted like grass. The lady working there was very nice and even explained where the matcha comes from and how they make it! We also tried the kimchi bacon garlic roll, which had a very interesting flavor that we both enjoyed. We got the buttery pressed croissant as well, but neither of us liked it—though to be fair, we aren't huge croissant fans anyway, so who knows! Finally, we picked up a couple of cream puffs, and the Biscoff crunch cream puffs were definitely our favorite.

text.text:
I got completely obsessed with the croissants at this pastry shop in San Gabriel 🥐

When the sunlight pours into the café, it honestly feels sooo cozy.
The whole space has this minimal Korean-style aesthetic,
and it’s the kind of place where just sitting there with a coffee feels super chill ✨

But the REAL highlight this time was definitely their croissants!!

I personally became obsessed with the pressed croissant 🥹
After being pressed flat, the outside becomes extra crispy,
and every bite has those super satisfying flaky layers.
The more you chew, the more fragrant it gets — honestly impossible to stop eating.

The kimchi bacon garlic roll with runny egg yolk was also such a surprise!
Even after reheating it at home, the egg yolk was still perfectly runny 🍳
The moment you cut it open = instant happiness hahaha.
Kimchi + bacon + garlic together worked SO well,
savory with just a slight spicy kick… seriously addictive.

The Dubai pistachio croissant had such a strong pistachio flavor,
and the black truffle mushroom croissant had an incredibly rich truffle aroma 🤎

For drinks, we ordered an Americano topped with pistachio cream
and a latte topped with black sesame cream ☕️

The cream toppings were super thick and flavorful!
They paired perfectly with both the croissants 🥐 and cream puffs.

Lastly, we took home a box of 8 different cream puffs.
Every single one was packed with filling — dessert lovers would seriously lose their minds here 🤣I got completely obsessed with the croissants at this pastry shop in San Gabriel 🥐

text.text:
Find me a treasure bread coffee shop with good looks and strength online!

As soon as I entered the door, I was confused by the box full of puffs and coson fragrance. It was completely a paradise for dessert fans✨

🧁Puff

Want 👍 their  Dong Ding Oolong and matcha puffs

Dong Ding Oolong

The tea fragrance is particularly strong, not that kind of dead sweet cream. At first, there is a faint oolong tea fragrance in the mouth, and the afterter sweetness is very obvious. After eating, it will not be greasy at all.

Matcha Puff

The source of happiness of matcha stars 🍵, the filling is thick and smooth, with a slight bitter charm and tea fragrance, and it goes well with the crispy puff shell.

🥐bread

Rose Lychee Raspberry Croffin
What is amazing this time is that the rose lychee is praised. The outer layer is crispy and the slag is falling off. The song itself has a rich milky fragrance, with the fragrance of rose and lychee fruit. The sense of hierarchy is particularly rich, which is a taste that will be remembered after a bite.

almond cream is also with a strong almond fragrance and silky milk sauce, which is full of aroma but not too sweet.

runny yolk kimchi bacon garlic roll
can be praised

Salty 👍 The moment you cut it, the egg yolk slowly flows out, with smoked flavor and garlic cream, salty and fragrant, which is very suitable for brunch.

☕️coffee

Their latte series is also very good.

Hazelnut Latte

With the aroma of nuts, the coffee and milk fragrance blend very smoothly.

Matcha Latte

The tea is rich in flavor, and the taste is silky and smooth. It is not only sweet, and matcha has a strong sense of presence.
Black Sesame Latte

The aroma of sesame is strong, and it feels like a combination of Chinese dessert and coffee. The more you drink, the more delicious it is.

On the whole, whether it is puffs, songs or drinks, it doesn't step on a mine. It's good-looking and delicious. It's very suitable for weekend dates, sister afternoon tea or being alone in a daze

Browned is located on the first floor of the one apartment building opposite the Hawaii supermarket. 🆓🅿️ It is very convenient.

text.text:
Everything here is amazing! The cream puffs are light and fresh, filled with perfectly balanced cream that isn’t too sweet. The croissants are buttery, flaky, and taste like they just came out of the oven. I also tried a few savory items, and they were just as impressive, flavorful and satisfying.

My favorites are the kimchi bacon and black truffle mushroom savories, plus the black sesame cream top and hazelnut latte.

The drinks are a highlight too. The hot ones are rich and comforting, while the cold drinks are refreshing and well-crafted. You can really tell they put care into everything they serve. Definitely one of my favorite spots!
